title: Steve Carell and his wife, Nancy, celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Steve Carell and wife Nancy celebrated her 60th birthday at Tresca in Boston's North End.
Nancy Carell, born in Cohasset and a Boston College graduate, runs a general store in Marshfield.
The couple met at Second City Theater in Chicago and married in 1995, both appearing in several films and shows.

title: Some Saugus residents stunned by high water bills, with one nearing $200,000
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Saugus residents report unusually high water bills, with one bill reaching nearly $200,000, prompting town investigation.
The issue began in March during the town's switch to new water meters and billing systems, causing unclear billing errors.
Homeowners facing penalties and liens for unpaid bills as legal and town efforts continue to address and resolve the situation.

title: Review & setlist: Bob Dylan was spookily stellar on a smoky night in Boston
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bob Dylan performed at Leader Bank Pavilion in Boston on July 16, 2026, featuring a mix of recent and a few classic songs.
The 85-year-old legend's performance included songs from his last 40 years and rare renditions from the 1960s.
Dylan's setlist included a mix of his own compositions and covers, supported by a stellar band lineup.

title: The middle seat will become a table on United’s newest Airbus jets
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines is introducing a middle seat turned into a table on new Airbus A321XLR aircraft.
The table will be available in Economy Plus with extra space, stretching from armrest to armrest.
Domestic flights on the new aircraft begin this fall, with international service starting early 2027.

title: Original ‘Gilmore Girls’ house is on the market, and it’s not in Stars Hollow
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The original 'Gilmore Girls' house from the pilot is for sale in Markham, Ontario, for $2.76 million USD.
Built around 1875, the 2,840-square-foot Gothic Revival home features four bedrooms and was modernized in the 1950s.
A 550-square-foot barn with historical ties as a village bakery is included on the property's grounds.

title: Longtime Maine soccer coach recognized by FIFA as ‘Unsung Hero’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Gary Newell, a Maine soccer coach, was named FIFA's 'Unsung Hero' during the 2026 World Cup at Gillette Stadium.
Newell was recognized for decades of contributions to youth soccer, receiving a standing ovation and a surprise video message from England's Jude Bellingham.
The award highlights Newell's impact in promoting soccer, describing it as the embodiment of love, passion, and making a difference.

title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old weeping willow collapsed in the Boston Public Garden, exceeding its typical lifespan of 30-50 years.
No injuries reported as the Friends of the Public Garden deemed the tree structurally sound during recent inspections.
Routine winter removals and spring replanting conducted to manage aging willow trees in the Boston Public Garden.

title: These new speed boards in Jamaica Plain, on the Esplanade track cyclists’ speeds, not just cars
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: DCR installed speed boards in Jamaica Plain and Esplanade to reduce cyclist and vehicle speeds on paths.
The boards collect data on user volume to assess path usage, responding to safety concerns from residents.
New installations focus on cyclist speeds only in areas like Memorial Drive, Neponset River Reservation, and Cape Cod Rail Trail.

title: Thousands demand Wu address street safety after cyclist killed in crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Thousands demand action from Mayor Wu after Louisa Gag, a transportation planner, was killed cycling on Tremont Street.
Activists call for stalled street-safety projects to proceed and urge city to appoint a permanent chief of streets.
Mayor Wu announced measures including increased enforcement of bike lane violations and allocating senior staff for safety project planning.

title: Police investigating after man, woman in their 70s found dead in Maine home
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Sagadahoc County Sheriff’s Department found Carolyn Harris, 77, and Kevin Harris, 75, dead in their West Bath home.
Maine State Police are investigating the deaths and conducting autopsies to determine the cause.
Officials state there is no danger to the public following the discovery of the bodies.
